title: Curated mobility-move catalog for the mobility log
description: ~26 mobility/flexibility moves at the HABIT tier (one move = one tap) for the mobility log (issue #840), each with a stable slug (an activity `components` entry), a kind (hold/reps), the #735 MuscleId(s) it mobilizes (rolled up to MuscleRegion for the coverage strip — a SEPARATE view from strength coverage, #482), and a how-to description. Committed + HUMAN-REVIEWABLE. Regenerate with `npm run gen:mobility-moves`. INFORMATIONAL movement guidance, NOT a PT/rehab prescription.

citation.note: Move selection and how-to descriptions reflect general mobility guidance; groupings are curated and human-reviewable, not medical advice.

entries.name: Neck CARs
entries.description: 5 slow controlled neck circles each direction, chin tracing the widest pain-free path

entries.name: Shoulder CARs
entries.description: 5 slow controlled shoulder circles each side, drawing the biggest circle you own

entries.name: Shoulder dislocates
entries.description: 10 passes taking a band or dowel from front to back overhead with straight arms

entries.name: Wall slides
entries.description: 10 reps sliding arms up and down a wall, keeping wrists and elbows in contact

entries.name: Thread the needle
entries.description: 8 reps per side on all fours, threading one arm under the body and reaching through

entries.name: Thoracic rotation
entries.description: 8 open-book rotations per side, following the top hand with your eyes

entries.name: Cat-cow
entries.description: 10 slow rounds on all fours, arching and rounding the whole spine with the breath

entries.name: Child's pose
entries.description: Hold 30–60s sitting hips to heels, arms reaching long, chest sinking toward the floor

entries.name: Wrist CARs
entries.description: 8 slow wrist circles each direction, then 5 gentle flexion/extension holds

entries.name: Hip flexor stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–60s per side in a half-kneel, tucking the pelvis and driving the hip forward

entries.name: Couch stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–60s per side, rear shin up a wall in a tall half-kneel, ribs down

entries.name: Pigeon pose
entries.description: Hold 45–60s per side, front shin across, back leg long, folding over the front hip

entries.name: Figure-four stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–45s per side on your back, ankle across the opposite knee, drawing the thigh in

entries.name: 90/90 hip switch
entries.description: 10 switches rotating both shins between 90/90 positions, staying tall through the spine

entries.name: Adductor rock-back
entries.description: 10 rock-backs with one leg out to the side on all fours, sinking into the inner thigh

entries.name: Deep squat hold
entries.description: Hold 45–60s in a flat-foot deep squat, elbows prying the knees out, chest up

entries.name: Glute bridge
entries.description: 12 reps on your back driving hips to the ceiling, squeezing the glutes at the top

entries.name: Hamstring stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–45s per side, heel forward and hips hinged, reaching toward the toes

entries.name: Seated forward fold
entries.description: Hold 45–60s seated with legs long, hinging from the hips and reaching for the feet

entries.name: Downward dog
entries.description: Hold 30–45s in an inverted-V, alternately pressing each heel toward the floor

entries.name: World's greatest stretch
entries.description: 5 reps per side in a lunge, dropping the elbow inside the front foot then rotating open

entries.name: Standing quad stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–45s per side, heel to glute and knees together, pelvis gently tucked

entries.name: Cossack squat
entries.description: 8 reps per side shifting side to side, one knee bent deep and the other leg straight

entries.name: Calf stretch
entries.description: Hold 30–45s per side, rear heel down in a split stance driving the knee over the toes

entries.name: Ankle rocks
entries.description: 10 rocks per side in a half-kneel, driving the front knee forward over the toes, heel down

entries.name: Dead hang
entries.description: Hang 20–45s from a bar with a relaxed grip, letting the shoulders and spine decompress
